name: hUC-MSC exosome osteoarthritis pilot showed uncontrolled dose signals
summary: A dose-randomized hUC-MSC exosome pilot reported safety and within-group WOMAC/MRI improvements, but lacked a placebo or standard-care control arm.
statement: In 41 knee osteoarthritis patients, hUC-MSC-derived exosome injections were reported as safe and associated with within-dose-group WOMAC and selected MRI improvements, but the pilot had no placebo control group and no significant efficacy differences among dose groups at matched time points.
population_or_model: Adults with mild to moderate knee osteoarthritis
time_horizon: Three intra-articular injections on days 0, 21, and 42 with up to 9-month follow-up
quantitative_note: The paper reported 45 affected limbs assigned across low-, medium-, and high-dose groups; authors noted small sample size, no control group, and partial MRI follow-up.
caveats: Dose randomization does not substitute for a placebo or standard-care comparator.
Within-group improvement is vulnerable to regression to the mean, placebo effects, and natural-history confounding.
The positive signal conflicts with a separate placebo-controlled placental MSC-EV osteoarthritis trial that was null.
